plan_metadata.name: Sample Dice Roller in meeting Local Debug

steps.description: Type text into the search bar within the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface: 'Dice Roller in meeting'.
Click the blue "Create" button next to "Dice Roller in meeting" in the Samples section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selected default folder path ("home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects") in the "Workspace Folder" dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
@assertion 'Preview README.MD' is visible
@assertion the page title contains 'Dice Roller - Live Share sample'
Perform a right-click on the "Preview README.md" tab in the Visual Studio Code interface at coordinates (397, 54), located in the upper section of the editor where open file tabs are displayed.
Click the "Close" option in the context menu within the Visual Studio Code interface to close the current file tab.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code, with the "LIVE-SHARE-DICE-ROLLER" project loaded in the Explorer view.
Type text into the command palette in Visual Studio Code: 'View: show microsoft'.
Press 'Enter' to select the 'View: Show Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit' option in the VS Code command palette.
Press the `F1` key to open the Command Palette in the Visual Studio Code interface, where the project context is labeled as "live-share-dice-roller."
Type text into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette: 'Microsoft 365 Agents: Accounts'.
Click on the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Accounts" option listed under the command palette in Visual Studio Code, specifically at the coordinates (266, 84).
Click on the "Sign in to Microsoft 365" option within the dropdown menu in the Visual Studio Code interface's top search bar.
Click the "Sign in" button within the dialog titled "Create a Microsoft 365 developer sandbox" to authenticate with a Microsoft 365 account.
Type the text ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}} into the input field labeled 'Email or phone' on the Microsoft sign-in page.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the email address entered in the Microsoft login form and proceed by activating the "Next" button on the webpage.
Type text: ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the 'Password' field on the Microsoft login page, identified by the header "Enter password" and the email address "test003@xxbdw.onmicrosoft.com".
Press the "Enter" key to submit the password form on the Microsoft login page, interacting with the "Sign in" button.
Click the "Close Tab" button (red 'X') in the browser's tab bar to close the "M365 Account - Sign In" page.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
Type the text 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging' into the Visual Studio Code command palette, which is opened with the prompt "Type the name of a command to run."
Press the "Enter" key to select the highlighted command, "Debug: Select and Start Debugging," from the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Type text: 'Debug in Teams (Chrome)' into the launch configuration dropdown within the Visual Studio Code Debug interface, targeting the "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" option.
Press the Enter key to select "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" from the dropdown menu in the Visual Studio Code debug configuration interface.
Click the "Add" button in the pop-up window for the TeamsConversationBot app on the Microsoft Teams interface.
Click the "testing" meeting option under "Select a meeting" in the popup titled "DiceRoller.LiveSharelocal" within the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Go" button in the DiceRoller.LiveSharelocal confirmation pop-up to proceed with app initialization.
Click the "Continue" button within the "You are about to connect to a developer tunnel" warning dialog in Microsoft Teams to proceed with connecting to the specified tunnel.
Click the "Save" button located in the Dice Roller interface within Microsoft Teams, positioned at the bottom-right of the dialog box.
Click the "Join" button in the DiceRollerFluidLiveShare tab within the Microsoft Teams web app, located in the "testing" chat window.
Click the "Join now" button in the Teams meeting interface to proceed with joining the session.
Click the "Allow" button in the browser prompt titled "teams.microsoft.com wants to" to grant permission to manage windows across displays.
Click the "Share" button located at the bottom-right corner of the DiceRollerFluidLiveShare panel to share the Dice Roller content in the Microsoft Teams meeting.
@assertion 'Roll' is visible
